response: the restaurant owner wants to transform their establishment into a tranquil oasis for diners. to achieve this, they plan to redesign the dining area and incorporate a small zen garden in the center. the dining area is currently a rectangular space measuring 20 meters in length and 15 meters in width. the zen garden will be a circular area with a radius of $ r $ meters, and it will be placed exactly in the center of the dining area. the owner wants the total area available for dining tables to be twice the area of the zen garden. calculate the radius $ r $ of the zen garden. the total area of the dining area is $ 20 \times 15 = 300 $ square meters. the area of the zen garden is $ \pi r^2 $. the area available for dining tables is $ 300 - \pi r^2 $. the owner wants this area to be twice the area of the zen garden, so $ 300 - \pi r^2 = 2 \pi r^2 $. solving this equation: $ 300 = 3 \pi r^2 $, $ r^2 = \frac{300}{3 \pi} $, $ r = \sqrt{\frac{100}{\pi}} $. the radius $ r $ of the zen garden is $ \sqrt{\frac{100}{\pi}} $ meters. the owner decides to install a small fountain at the center of the zen garden. the fountain will be a cylinder with a height of 1.5 meters and a radius that is one-third of the zen garden's radius. the radius of the fountain is $ \frac{r}{3} $. the surface area of the cylindrical fountain is $ 2 \pi \times \left(\frac{r}{3}\right) \times 1.5 $. the cost to cover the surface area of the cylindrical fountain is $ 50 \times 2 \pi \times \left(\frac{r}{3}\right) \times 1.5 $. the total cost to cover the surface area of the cylindrical fountain is $ 50 \times 2 \pi \times \left(\frac{r}{3}\right) \times 1.5 $. the pen is mightier than sword. the pen is mightier than sword. the pen is mightier than sword. the pen is mightier than sword. the pen is mightier than
